research.interface: DynamoDB API 2012-08-10 via AWS SDK for Go v2 (service/dynamodb, feature/dynamodb/attributevalue)

official_sources.title: Query - Amazon DynamoDB
official_sources.used_for: KeyConditionExpression format (partition equality plus sort-key comparison), ExpressionAttributeValues with :placeholder AttributeValue maps, ExclusiveStartKey/LastEvaluatedKey pagination semantics (only an empty LastEvaluatedKey means the last page; an empty page can still carry one), Limit behavior, ReturnConsumedCapacity TOTAL and the ConsumedCapacity response shape, and throttling errors (ProvisionedThroughputExceededException, ThrottlingException)

official_sources.title: BatchWriteItem - Amazon DynamoDB
official_sources.used_for: 25-item / 16 MB batch limits, RequestItems shape (table -> WriteRequest with PutRequest.Item), UnprocessedItems having the same shape as RequestItems and the documented guidance to resend them with exponential backoff, non-atomicity of the batch, whole-call ProvisionedThroughputExceededException when nothing was processed, and per-table ConsumedCapacity list in the response

official_sources.title: attributevalue package - github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/feature/dynamodb/attributevalue - Go Packages
official_sources.used_for: official Go v2 marshaling surface: Marshal/MarshalMap to types.AttributeValue, UnmarshalListOfMaps for Query results, and dynamodbav struct tags mapping Go fields to attribute names

research.language_rationale: aws-sdk-go-v2 is AWS's current official Go SDK (v1 is in maintenance mode); service/dynamodb is the documented client for the DynamoDB JSON API and feature/dynamodb/attributevalue is its official marshaling layer. The exporter is written against a narrow consumer-side interface satisfied by *dynamodb.Client, and the tests inject a fake that exchanges the SDK's real input/output/types values.

verified_facts: Query requires an equality test on the partition key in KeyConditionExpression; a sort-key range condition is combined with AND, and :placeholders are bound via ExpressionAttributeValues maps of AttributeValue.

Query paginates by returning LastEvaluatedKey; the caller passes it verbatim as ExclusiveStartKey. Only an empty LastEvaluatedKey means the result set is complete - a page with zero items can still carry a non-empty key.

ConsumedCapacity is only returned when ReturnConsumedCapacity is set; TOTAL returns the aggregate with CapacityUnits. Query responses carry one ConsumedCapacity object; BatchWriteItem responses carry a list with one entry per table.
BatchWriteItem accepts at most 25 put/delete operations (16 MB total) per call and is not atomic as a whole; individual puts are.

Failed operations come back in UnprocessedItems, which has the same shape as RequestItems so it can be resent directly; AWS strongly recommends exponential backoff when retrying, because immediate retries are likely to be throttled again.

If none of the batch items can be processed on a provisioned table, BatchWriteItem fails the whole call with ProvisionedThroughputExceededException; other throttling scenarios surface ThrottlingException.
The Go v2 attributevalue package marshals Go structs to map[string]types.AttributeValue using dynamodbav struct tags (Marshal/MarshalMap) and decodes Query/Scan pages with UnmarshalListOfMaps.
